behavior_summary: This script group provides per-item/book handlers that prepare and present discrete blocks of lore/text to the game's book-reading UI. Each handler builds a temporary string containing the book text, sets book context/info, invokes Book::read to display the text to the player/actor, frees the temporary string, and returns Book::read's result.
Handlers do not read or write global flags in this excerpt; their behavior is local to the call sequence and temporary string management.
Multiple helper functions correspond to different book entries (e.g., Expedition Journal, De-venoming the Kith, poems), all following the same allocate/push/context/call/free/return pattern.
notable_control_flow: Allocate or convert a literal into a temporary string (string_temp).
Push context values (a constant 0x0001, the invoking actor/object, an indirect info pointer) and call an engine 'set info' style operation prior to reading.
Push pid and the prepared text string, call Book::read(text) to present UI and receive a retval.
Free the temporary string and return Book::read's retval to the caller.
open_questions: What is the exact semantic meaning of the indirect 2-byte info pointer pushed before calling set info (a book metadata record? formatting flags?), and how Book::read consumes it?
What is the precise role of the constant 0x0001 pushed before the context—an access/permission flag, mode, or UI option?
Is the BP+06h parameter always the actor PID, the book object instance, or sometimes either depending on caller?
Does Book::read perform asynchronous UI suspension (suspend) or block until the player finishes reading, and does it return discrete status codes that downstream logic inspects?

main_function.name: U8P_STEVBOOK::0080
main_function.purpose: Prepare book context and pass a block of lore text to Book::read so the player/actor can read it; free temporary resources and return the read routine's result.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the associated book/item is activated/read by the player or relevant actor (inferred from the set-info + Book::read call pattern).

function_map.name: U8P_STEVBOOK::0080
function_map.role: Primary handler that sets up book context, allocates the book text string, calls Book::read, frees the string, and returns Book::read's retval.

function_map.name: U8P_STEVBOOK::07D7
function_map.role: Helper handler: prepare and present the 'Expedition Journal, Book II' text via Book::read (same control pattern as main).

function_map.name: U8P_STEVBOOK::10E5
function_map.role: Helper handler: prepare and present 'De-venoming the Kith' text via Book::read.

function_map.name: U8P_STEVBOOK::17FE
function_map.role: Helper handler: present the poem 'The Beauty of Rhian' via Book::read.

function_map.name: U8P_STEVBOOK::1965
function_map.role: Helper handler: present 'The Voices of Mary' lyrical entry via Book::read.

function_map.name: U8P_STEVBOOK::1D0E
function_map.role: Helper handler: present a long lore entry ('Eye of the Boulder...') via Book::read.

function_map.name: U8P_STEVBOOK::2650
function_map.role: Helper handler: present a short report/lore entry (Captain's report) via Book::read.

function_map.name: Book::read
function_map.role: Engine/UI routine that displays the provided text string in the book-reading UI/flow and returns a status/result.

external_calls.purpose: Display the prepared text string in the book-reading UI using the previously pushed context (actor/object, info pointer, pid); returns a status value that is forwarded to the caller.
Present the 'Expedition Journal, Book II' text to the player using Book::read.
Present the 'De-venoming the Kith' instructional text to the player.
Present 'The Beauty of Rhian' poem text to the player.
Present 'The Voices of Mary' lyrical entry.
Present the long lore entry 'Eye of the Boulder, the Runes of the Myth Drainer'.
Present the short Captain's report via Book::read.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script group provides per-item/book handlers that prepare and present discrete blocks of lore/text to the game's book-reading UI. Each handler builds a temporary string containing the book text, sets book context/info, invokes Book::read to display the text to the player/actor, frees the temporary string, and returns Book::read's result.
